entity_name: U.S. Government Critical Minerals Funding List (2023-2026)
canonical_name: Critical Minerals Institute Funding Master Table
facts: The U.S. government has moved from policy statements to capital deployment using multiple federal pipes to accelerate domestic and allied supply chains for critical minerals and downstream manufacturing.

Funding flows through five key U.S. government divisions: DOE (notably LPO), DoD (including DPA Title III and Office of Strategic Capital), Commerce (CHIPS Program Office), USDA Rural Development, and DFC, plus EXIM as a major financing engine.
The list was compiled by the Critical Minerals Institute (CMI) and publishes exclusively on InvestorNews.com to serve as a living reference for investors, industry participants, and policymakers.
6K Additive, Inc. is a private company that received a DoD DPAI award of US$23.4M announced on Dec 5, 2023.
The DoD award expands domestic capability to upcycle waste/scrap into higher-grade metals for defense and commercial supply chains.
Frank Roberts serves as CEO & Managing Director of 6K Additive.
6K Additive produces high-performance metal powders for additive manufacturing and alloy additions using UniMelt® technology from sustainable sources.
The company targets applications in aerospace, defense, medical, automotive, and industrial markets.

The award explicitly supports turning domestic scrap into high-grade titanium, niobium, nickel, and tungsten production capacity for U.S. defense applications including aircraft structures, turbine blades, rocket engines, and radar.
Albemarle Corporation is a public company with multiple U.S. government funding awards.
Albemarle received a DOE selection/award of US$67.1M under the Battery Materials Processing/Battery Manufacturing program announced on Sep 20, 2024.
The DOE award supports an anode-materials initiative in North Carolina for lithium metal anode (LMA) materials to increase battery energy density.
Albemarle received a DoD DPA Title III award of US$90M announced on Sep 12, 2023 to expand U.S. lithium production and domestic battery supply chains.
The DoD award is tied to restarting the Kings Mountain, North Carolina mine and funding a fleet of mining equipment as hard-capex.
Albemarle received a DOE grant of US$150M announced on Oct 19, 2022 tied to its Kings Mountain, North Carolina lithium facility supporting the domestic EV supply chain.
Kent Masters serves as CEO & Chairman of Albemarle.
Albemarle is a global leader in transforming lithium and bromine into critical ingredients for mobility, energy, connectivity, and health.
Altona Rare Earths Plc is a public company receiving USTDA support for the Monte Muambe Rare Earths Project with a grant agreement in preparation and undisclosed amount.
USTDA confirmed its intention to support the project on Feb 9, 2026, following confirmation delivered Feb 8, 2026 in Cape Town.
Cédric Simonet serves as CEO & Director of Altona Rare Earths.
The Monte Muambe Project in northwest Mozambique hosts rare earths, fluorspar, and gallium mineralisation.
Altona completed over 7,800 metres of drilling and delivered a maiden JORC Mineral Resource Estimate of 13.6 Mt at 2.42% total rare earth oxides.
The company secured a 25-year mining licence in December 2024 and published a Competent Person Report and scoping study in October 2023.
Altona targets 50,000 tonnes per annum of high-grade acid-grade fluorspar production over a minimum 12-year mine life.
Gallium recovery is planned as a by-product of fluorspar tailings.
The USTDA confirmation aligned with the 2026 Critical Minerals Ministerial and the launch of FORGE to strengthen resilient critical minerals supply chains.
Amaero Ltd. is a public company that signed an EXIM credit agreement (loan) of US$22.8M announced on Feb 26, 2025.
The financing supports U.S. expansion and capacity buildout.
Hank J. Holland serves as CEO & Executive Chairman of Amaero.
Amaero has manufacturing and corporate headquarters in Tennessee, USA.
The company produces high-value C103, refractory alloy, and titanium powders for additive and advanced manufacturing for defense, space, and aviation industries.
Amaero uses advanced gas atomization technology with an industry-leading yield of AM powder.
The company is a leader in PM-HIP manufacturing of large, near-net-shape powder metallurgy parts.
The facility was originally announced as US$23.5M but decreased to US$22.8M after EXIM reduced certain capitalized fees.
American Battery Technology Company (ABAT) is a public company that received an EXIM Letter of Interest (non-binding) for up to US$900M announced on Apr 29, 2025.
The LOI supports financing tied to the Tonopah Flats Lithium Project in Nevada.
Ryan Melsert serves as CEO & Director of ABAT.
ABTC is headquartered in Reno, Nevada and pioneers technologies for domestically manufactured and recycled battery metals for EV, stationary storage, and consumer electronics.
Tonopah Flats encompasses one of the largest known measured/indicated/inferred lithium claystone resource deposits in the U.S.
Proven & Probable reserves are estimated at ~559,848 metric tonnes at an average grade of 805 ppm lithium.
American Rare Earths Limited is a public company that received an EXIM Letter of Interest (non-binding) for up to US$456M announced on Sep 24, 2024.
The LOI covers the construction/execution phase of the Cowboy State Mine area at the Halleck Creek Project with a referenced 15-year repayment term under EXIM’s Make More in America initiative.
Mark Wall serves as CEO & Executive Director of American Rare Earths.
The company advances the Halleck Creek Project in Wyoming through its wholly owned subsidiary, Wyoming Rare (USA) Inc.
Halleck Creek is a world-class rare earth deposit with plans for onsite mineral processing and separation facilities to reduce U.S. reliance on imports from China.
American Rare Earths collaborates with U.S. Government-supported R&D programs to develop innovative extraction and processing technologies for rare earth elements.
Anovion Technologies is a private company that received a DOE BIL grant of US$117M under the Battery Materials Processing & Manufacturing program announced on Oct 19, 2022.
The grant supports construction of a large-scale factory in Bainbridge, Georgia and investment in a relocated Center of Excellence in Sanborn, New York.
Anovion targets roughly 150,000 tpa of anode-grade synthetic graphite capacity by 2030.
Anovion also received an EXIM LOI for up to US$400M announced on Nov 7, 2024 for direct project financing.
Clay Kiefaber serves as CEO & Advisory Board Member of Anovion.
Anovion is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois and brings over 140 years of experience in synthetic graphite materials.
Anovion’s products were the first made in North America to gain qualification for EV applications with commercial production commencing in early 2021.
In October 2025, the Trump DOE confirmed canceling more than US$700M of earlier clean-manufacturing grants including Anovion’s, though only about US$13.8M had been disbursed.
The company continued planning its Georgia facility and broader expansion despite the cancellation.

Ares Strategic Mining Inc. is a public company awarded a DoD DLA IDIQ contract for acid-grade fluorspar with an award date of Dec 29, 2025 (posted Jan 13, 2026) and company announcement on Jan 20, 2026.
The reported award is US$168,938,267 with a stated contract ceiling up to US$250M under the five-year framework.
James Walker serves as President, CEO & Director of Ares Strategic Mining.
The contract ties to Award ID SP8000-26-D-0002 (DoD/DLA, DCSO Fort Belvoir).
Ascend Elements, Inc. is a private company that received a DOE BIL cost-share grant of ~US$316M for the Apex 1 pCAM/CAM facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky announced on Oct 19, 2022.
Ascend Elements also received a DOE MESC Advanced Graphite Recycling cost-share grant of US$125M with Orbia’s Koura business announced on Sep 23, 2024.
Linh Austin serves as President, CEO & Director of Ascend Elements.

The company is headquartered in Westborough, Massachusetts and uses its patented Hydro-to-Cathode process to convert end-of-life batteries into pCAM, cathode active materials, and battery-grade lithium carbonate.
More than US$200M had already been disbursed when the DOE moved to rescind the remaining funds in the 2025 review.
Ascend plans to complete the Kentucky plant using a mix of equity, project finance, municipal bonds, and other debt.
Critical Metals Corp is a public company that received an EXIM Letter of Interest (non-binding) for up to US$120M with a referenced 15-year term announced on Jun 16, 2025.
Tony Sage serves as CEO & Executive Chairman of Critical Metals Corp.
The company’s flagship Tanbreez project is one of the world’s largest rare earth deposits located in Southern Greenland.
Critical Metals also owns the Wolfsberg Lithium Project in Carinthia, Austria, which is the first fully permitted mine in Europe.
Reuters described the EXIM LOI as the Trump administration’s first overseas mining investment.
EnergySource Minerals LLC is a private company that received a DOE LPO conditional commitment (direct loan) of up to US$1.36B announced on Jan 15, 2025.
The borrower is SPV ESM ATLiS LLC with a structure of US$1.22B principal plus US$141M capitalized interest.
Eric Spomer serves as President & CEO of EnergySource Minerals.
Proceeds will finance construction/equipping/operation of a lithium hydroxide facility in Imperial County, California producing lithium from geothermal brine via direct lithium extraction (DLE).
DOE flags potential co-products including zinc and manganese.
Project ATLiS is expected to be the first facility in the U.S. to use DLE at commercial scale as a closed-loop power-plant-to-lithium system.
Fireweed Metals Corp is a public company that received a DoD DPA Title III award of US$15.8M announced on Dec 13, 2024.
Ian Gibbs serves as President, CEO & Director of Fireweed Metals.
The award advances the 100%-owned Mactung tungsten project toward a final investment decision.
source_title: Follow the Money: The U.S. Government Funding Hit List for Critical Minerals Companies (2023–2026) - InvestorNews
